title: Servagent Mission
description: Structured execution plan generated by a client (typically Claude Desktop) and executed by the Servagent daemon on a VPS. Missions are immutable once submitted: an execution produces an audit trail, never a modification of the source plan.

schema_version.description: Version of this mission schema. The agent rejects missions whose schema_version it does not support. Incremented on any breaking change.

mission_id.description: Unique identifier for this mission. Generated by the client. Used as the primary key in the audit log and as a reference target for resume plans. Convention: kebab-case, descriptive prefix, short entropy suffix (e.g. 'deploy-monapp-2026-04-20-abc123').

parent_mission_id.description: If this mission is a retry, resume, or follow-up of a previous one, reference its mission_id here. Used to reconstruct execution chains in the audit log.

created_by.description: Identifier of the client that generated this plan. Informational only — authentication is enforced at the transport layer (bearer token + IP whitelist).

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC recommended) at which the plan was generated.

goal.description: One-sentence human-readable intent of the mission. Displayed to the user during validation and logged verbatim. Not parsed.

mode.description: Execution mode. 'execute' runs the mission for real (default). 'dry_run' reports what each step would do without producing side effects (steps classified as observation-only still run; modifying steps return a simulated result).

context.description: Free-form metadata describing the environment the mission operates on. Purely informational — the agent does not route on this field. Typical keys: vps, domain, repo, stack, environment.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps to execute. Execution is sequential by array order. A step may be skipped at runtime if any step it depends_on has failed.

id.description: Unique identifier for this step within the mission. Used as target of depends_on and in audit logs.

tool.description: Name of the atomic tool to invoke. Must match a tool registered in the agent's library. The agent rejects the mission at submission time if any tool is unknown.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this step's intent. Displayed to the user during plan validation. Useful when the tool name alone is cryptic.

params.description: Parameters passed to the tool. Shape is validated by the tool's own JSON Schema at execution time, not here. May contain ${var} or ${var.path} interpolation references to output_var values set by earlier steps.

depends_on.description: IDs of earlier steps that must have completed successfully before this step runs. If any dependency failed (or was itself skipped), this step is skipped. Must reference steps that appear earlier in the steps array.

output_var.description: Name under which this step's structured output is stored. Referenced in later steps via ${name} or ${name.path.to.value}. Variable names are scoped to the mission.

on_error.description: Behavior on step failure. 'abort' (default): halt the mission. 'continue': log and move to the next step. 'retry': re-run per the retry policy, then abort if still failing. 'escalate': pause the mission and notify the client for replanning (support scheduled for v0.2).

retry.description: Retry policy, applicable only when on_error is 'retry'. Ignored otherwise.

max_attempts.description: Total attempts including the first one. Must be >= 2 (a single attempt is not a retry).

delay_seconds.description: Initial delay between attempts. For exponential backoff, delay is multiplied by 2 each retry.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum wall-clock time for this step. If exceeded, the step is considered failed and on_error applies. If omitted, the tool's own default applies.
